2026-04-18T17:13:36.6083434Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T17:13:36.6083849Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T17:13:36.6084257Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T17:13:36.6084574Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T17:13:36.6084984Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-18T17:16:16.8209590Z 
2026-04-18T17:16:16.8209935Z ◆ Now I can see the issue. The CHANGELOG for `Azure.Storage.DataMovement.Blobs` 12.4.0-beta.1 mentions:
2026-04-18T17:16:16.8210786Z   - "Added support for snapshot transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a snapshot to a new blob, download a snapshot, and pause/resume snapshot transfers."
2026-04-18T17:16:16.8212105Z   - "Added support for versioning transfers as the source. This includes the ability to copy a version to a new blob, download a version, and pause/resume version transfers."
2026-04-18T17:16:16.8212723Z   
2026-04-18T17:16:16.8212922Z   But the README has no documentation for:

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created.
